264.reason: [Error: When encountering a single '&', the token should be created with the error type, but the code does not increment curPos after reading the '&', causing the next call to hasNext() to re-read the same '&' character, leading to an infinite loop or incorrect tokenization.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: When encountering a single '&', the token should be created with the error type, but the code does not increment curPos after reading the '&', causing the next call to hasNext() to re-read the same '&' character, leading to an infinite loop or incorrect tokenization.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: When encountering a single '&', the token should be an error token, but the program does not handle the case where curPos might be out of bounds after incrementing. Additionally, the error token's line number is correct, but the program fails to output the error because the error token's lexType is null and errorType is set, which is handled correctly in Compiler.java. However, the main issue is that the program does not handle the case where the source string ends with a single '&' or '|', causing an IndexOutOfBoundsException when accessing source.charAt(curPos) after incrementing curPos.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [This line creates a Token with ErrorType but uses the Token constructor that expects LexType, causing lexType to be null and errorType to be set; however, the Token constructor overload for ErrorType is defined, so this is actually correct. The real issue is missing bounds check before accessing source.charAt(curPos).]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [When encountering a single '&', the code creates an error token but does not advance curPos beyond the current character, which may cause infinite loops or incorrect reprocessing; more critically, it fails to check if curPos is within bounds before accessing source.charAt(curPos), leading to potential IndexOutOfBoundsException when '&' appears at the end of file.]

273.reason: [Error: Similarly, when encountering a single '|', the code does not increment curPos after reading the '|', causing the same issue as above.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: Similarly, when encountering a single '|', the code does not increment curPos after reading the '|', causing the same issue as above.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue as above: potential IndexOutOfBoundsException if source ends with a single '|'.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Same as above: missing bounds check before accessing source.charAt(curPos); if curPos is at the end of source, charAt will throw StringIndexOutOfBoundsException, but more critically, the logic fails to handle end-of-input correctly, leading to missed error reporting or crashes.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue as above: no bounds check before accessing source.charAt(curPos), and curPos is not advanced after recording the error, which can cause repeated processing of the same character or crash if '|' is the last character in the input.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.07692307692307693 , reason:  Missing boundary check: curPos+1 may exceed source.length(), causing StringIndexOutOfBoundsException

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.07692307692307693 , reason:  Recursive call ignores return value; should be 'return hasNext();' to properly propagate false when EOF is reached
